items.title: Introduction
items.description: Welcome to Schema Weaver — a PostgreSQL schema management platform with a visual SQL Editor, ER diagram, migration engine, Data Explorer, and AI assistant
items.content:
[Schema Weaver](https://schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) Documentation

Welcome to the official documentation for Schema Weaver. This guide covers everything from connecting your first PostgreSQL database to running migrations, exploring your data, and collaborating with your team.

---

What is Schema Weaver?

Schema Weaver is a PostgreSQL schema management platform. It gives you a visual SQL Editor for writing and organizing your schema, an auto-generated ER diagram that updates as you type, a mig
items.snippet: Welcome to the official documentation for Schema Weaver. This guide covers everything from connecting your first PostgreSQL database to running migrations, exploring your data, and collaborating with

items.title: Adding a Connection
items.description: Add a PostgreSQL connection to Schema Weaver — fill in credentials, choose an SSL mode, test the connection, and understand how credentials are stored securely
items.content:
Adding a Connection

All database connections in Schema Weaver are added from the SQL Editor. Once saved, a connection is available across both the SQL Editor (for schema migrations) and the Data Explorer (for browsing and querying your data).

> Quick links: [Database Overview](/database) · [Connection Dashboard](/database/connection-dashboard)

---

How to Add a Connection

1. Open the [SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/)
2. Click Connect in the footer status bar at the bottom
items.snippet: All database connections in Schema Weaver are added from the SQL Editor. Once saved, a connection is available across both the SQL Editor (for schema migrations) and the Data Explorer (for browsing an

items.title: Connection Dashboard
items.description: Manage your saved connections, link them to projects, monitor live activity, and use the connection panel in the SQL Editor sidebar

headings.title: Linking a Connection to a Project

items.content:
Connection Dashboard

Once you've added one or more connections, Schema Weaver gives you several views for managing and using them — a connections list, a sidebar panel while you're working in the SQL Editor, and a full connection dashboard for each connection.

> Quick links: [Adding a Connection](/database/adding-a-connection) · [Pull, Diff & Push](/database/pull-diff-push)

---

Connections List

After adding connections they appear in the connection panel. Open the panel from the SQL Editor
items.snippet: Once you've added one or more connections, Schema Weaver gives you several views for managing and using them — a connections list, a sidebar panel while you're working in the SQL Editor, and a full co

items.title: Drift Detection
items.description: Detect when your live PostgreSQL database has drifted from your project SQL — understand what causes drift, how Schema Weaver detects it, and how to resolve it

headings.title: Keep the external changes (they are intentional)
Discard the external changes (they should not have been made)

items.content:
Drift Detection

Drift happens when someone (or something) changes your live database's schema outside of Schema Weaver. Your project SQL says one thing, but the actual database looks different. Schema Weaver detects this automatically and prevents you from overwriting those changes unknowingly.

> Quick links: [Pull, Diff & Push](/database/pull-diff-push) · [Migrations](/database/migrations)

---

What Causes Drift

Drift can be introduced by:

- A developer running `ALTER TABLE` or `CREATE IND
items.snippet: Drift happens when someone (or something) changes your live database's schema outside of Schema Weaver. Your project SQL says one thing, but the actual database looks different. Schema Weaver detects

items.title: History & Rollback
items.description: View the full audit trail of schema migrations for each connection, and roll back applied migrations with automatically generated reverse SQL

headings.title: What Rollback Can and Cannot Undo

items.content:
History & Rollback

Every migration applied through Schema Weaver is recorded in a persistent history log — who ran it, when, and what the outcome was. If a migration causes a problem, you can roll it back with a single command. The reverse SQL is generated automatically at apply time, so rollback is always available.

> Quick links: [Migrations](/database/migrations) · [Terminal & sw Commands](/database/terminal)

---

Migration History

`sw history` lists all migrations applied through Schema
items.snippet: Every migration applied through Schema Weaver is recorded in a persistent history log — who ran it, when, and what the outcome was. If a migration causes a problem, you can roll it back with a single

items.title: Database
items.description: Connect Schema Weaver to your live PostgreSQL databases — manage connections, sync schemas, run migrations, detect drift, and explore your data across the SQL Editor and Data Explorer

headings.title: What You Can Do With a Connection
What Schema Weaver Reads From Your Database

items.content:
Database

Schema Weaver is built for PostgreSQL. Every live-database feature — schema sync, migration, drift detection, data exploration — works exclusively with PostgreSQL. This focus means every part of the system is purpose-built for PostgreSQL's type system, DDL model, catalog structure, and extension ecosystem.

> Quick links: [SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/)

---

Where Connections Live

All database connectio
items.snippet: Schema Weaver is built for PostgreSQL. Every live-database feature — schema sync, migration, drift detection, data exploration — works exclusively with PostgreSQL. This focus means every part of the s

items.title: Migrations
items.description: Apply schema changes to your live PostgreSQL database — understand the migration pipeline, use safe mode for complex type changes, and control destructive operations
items.content:
Migrations

`sw push --apply` applies your schema changes to the live database. Schema Weaver wraps every migration in a multi-step pipeline designed for correctness and safety — advisory locking to prevent concurrent changes, automatic drift checking before execution, and full transaction wrapping so partial failures roll back cleanly.

> Quick links: [Pull, Diff & Push](/database/pull-diff-push) · [Drift Detection](/database/drift-detection) · [History & Rollback](/database/history-rollback)
items.snippet: `sw push --apply` applies your schema changes to the live database. Schema Weaver wraps every migration in a multi-step pipeline designed for correctness and safety — advisory locking to prevent concu

items.title: Pull, Diff & Push
items.description: Sync your project SQL with your live PostgreSQL database — import the live schema with pull, compare differences with diff, and preview or apply changes with push
items.content:
Pull, Diff & Push

The core database workflow in Schema Weaver is three steps: pull the live schema into your project files, diff to see what has changed, and push to apply your changes back. These three operations keep your project SQL and your live PostgreSQL database in sync.

> Quick links: [Migrations](/database/migrations) · [Drift Detection](/database/drift-detection) · [Terminal & sw Commands](/database/terminal)

---

The Workflow



You can also start from scratch — write your schema f
items.snippet: The core database workflow in Schema Weaver is three steps: **pull** the live schema into your project files, **diff** to see what has changed, and **push** to apply your changes back. These three ope

items.title: Terminal & sw Commands
items.description: Run database operations directly from the built-in sw terminal in the SQL Editor — complete reference for all sw commands
items.content:
Terminal & sw Commands

The SQL Editor has a built-in terminal for running database operations directly without leaving the editor. Once a connection is active, open the terminal from the connection panel and run `sw` commands — a CLI for interacting with your connected PostgreSQL database.

> Quick links: [Pull, Diff & Push](/database/pull-diff-push) · [Migrations](/database/migrations) · [Drift Detection](/database/drift-detection)

![The built-in terminal showing sw status and sw pull command
items.snippet: The SQL Editor has a built-in terminal for running database operations directly without leaving the editor. Once a connection is active, open the terminal from the connection panel and run `sw` comman

items.title: AI Tools Reference
items.description: Complete reference for all 55 tools available to Resona AI in the SQL Editor — schema analysis, file management, database migrations, graph analysis, audits, and more
items.content:
AI Tools Reference

Resona AI has access to 55 purpose-built tools it can call autonomously when answering your questions. You never call these tools directly — Resona decides which ones to use based on your intent. This page is a complete reference so you know exactly what Resona is capable of.

> Quick links: [Resona AI Overview](/sql-editor/ai-assistant) · [Workspace Editing](/sql-editor/ai-assistant-workspace)

---

How Tools Work

When you send a message, Resona's agentic loop plans which t
items.snippet: Resona AI has access to **55 purpose-built tools** it can call autonomously when answering your questions. You never call these tools directly — Resona decides which ones to use based on your intent.

items.title: Workspace Editing
items.description: Resona AI makes targeted, surgical edits to your SQL project files — single-block patches, multi-change file edits, and cross-file refactoring with full preview and rollback
items.content:
Workspace Editing

When Resona AI edits your SQL files it does not regenerate entire files from scratch. It finds the exact block that needs to change and replaces only that — leaving everything else untouched. It can make multiple changes to a single file in one go, or apply edits across several files at once, with full rollback if anything goes wrong.

> Quick links: [Resona AI Overview](/sql-editor/ai-assistant) · [AI Tools Reference](/sql-editor/ai-assistant-tools) · [Version History](/sql-e
items.snippet: When Resona AI edits your SQL files it does not regenerate entire files from scratch. It finds the exact block that needs to change and replaces only that — leaving everything else untouched. It can m

items.title: Resona AI — SQL Editor
items.description: An agentic AI assistant built into the SQL Editor — generate DDL, analyze schemas, edit files across your project, run migrations, and reason through your entire schema with 50+ purpose-built tools
items.content:
Resona AI — SQL Editor

Resona AI is an agentic AI assistant built directly into the SQL Editor. It is not a simple chat interface that generates a code block for you to copy — it is a full agentic system with 55 purpose-built tools that can read your files, reason about your schema graph, run the compiler, analyze security and performance, write surgical multi-file edits, diff your live database, and apply migrations — all from a single conversation.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sq
items.snippet: Resona AI is an agentic AI assistant built directly into the SQL Editor. It is not a simple chat interface that generates a code block for you to copy — it is a full agentic system with 55 purpose-bui

items.title: Resona AI in the ER Diagram
items.description: Analyze tables, schema groups, and your entire schema with Resona AI directly from the ER Diagram — generate SQL, suggest indexes, check normalization, and apply changes instantly
items.content:
Resona AI in the ER Diagram

Resona AI is embedded directly into the ER Diagram at three levels: individual tables, schema groups, and the full schema. Each level opens a chat panel anchored by a dashed blue edge to its subject — you can ask questions, request SQL, and apply generated changes without leaving the diagram.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [ER Diagram Overview](/sql-editor/er-diagram) · [Resona AI Assistant](/sql-editor/ai-assistant)

---
items.snippet: Resona AI is embedded directly into the ER Diagram at three levels: individual tables, schema groups, and the full schema. Each level opens a chat panel anchored by a dashed blue edge to its subject —

items.title: Grouping & Schema Clusters
items.description: Cluster your ER diagram nodes by PostgreSQL schema namespace — resizable group containers, schema color coding, per-group stats, and lock mode
items.content:
Grouping & Schema Clusters

When Cluster by Schema is enabled, the ER Diagram groups all nodes by their PostgreSQL schema namespace into visually distinct containers. Tables, views, functions, and other objects that belong to `auth`, `billing`, `public`, `inventory` — or any other schema in your project — are enclosed in their own labeled group. This makes it immediately clear which domain each object belongs to and eliminates the visual noise of a flat diagram with hundreds of nodes.

> Quick l
items.snippet: When **Cluster by Schema** is enabled, the ER Diagram groups all nodes by their PostgreSQL schema namespace into visually distinct containers. Tables, views, functions, and other objects that belong t

items.title: Path Analysis
items.description: Find the shortest foreign-key join path between any two tables in your schema — with BFS and Dijkstra algorithms, visual highlighting, and confidence filtering
items.content:
Path Analysis

Path Analysis finds the shortest foreign-key relationship chain between any two tables in your schema. Instead of manually tracing JOIN paths through a dense diagram, you select a start table and an end table — the diagram highlights every intermediate table and edge you would need to traverse to connect them.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [ER Diagram Overview](/sql-editor/er-diagram)

![Path Analysis active in the ER Diagram showing
items.snippet: Path Analysis finds the **shortest foreign-key relationship chain** between any two tables in your schema. Instead of manually tracing JOIN paths through a dense diagram, you select a start table and

items.title: Visibility & Node Types
items.description: Control exactly which PostgreSQL object types and which schema namespaces are shown in the ER Diagram — declutter large schemas and focus on what matters
items.content:
Visibility & Node Types

The ER Diagram supports every PostgreSQL object type — tables, views, materialized views, functions, enums, domains, roles, sequences, extensions, and RLS policies. On a real production schema this can mean dozens or hundreds of extra nodes beyond your core tables. The Visibility controls let you show only what you need, making large diagrams readable and layout faster.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [ER Diagram Overview](/sq
items.snippet: The ER Diagram supports every PostgreSQL object type — tables, views, materialized views, functions, enums, domains, roles, sequences, extensions, and RLS policies. On a real production schema this ca

items.title: ER Diagram
items.description: Visualize your entire PostgreSQL schema as a live, interactive entity-relationship diagram — all object types supported, updates in real time as you write SQL
items.content:
ER Diagram

The ER Diagram is a live, interactive visualization of your entire PostgreSQL schema. Every object — tables, views, materialized views, functions, enums, domains, sequences, roles, extensions, and RLS policies — renders as a node on a pannable, zoomable canvas. Foreign-key relationships appear as directed edges. The diagram updates in real time as you type SQL with no manual refresh required, and scales to schemas with thousands of tables.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sq
items.snippet: The ER Diagram is a live, interactive visualization of your entire PostgreSQL schema. Every object — tables, views, materialized views, functions, enums, domains, sequences, roles, extensions, and RLS

items.title: File Manager
items.description: Organize your PostgreSQL schemas into cloud projects, folders, and files using the SQL Editor's built-in file manager
items.content:
File Manager

The SQL Editor's file manager lets you organize your PostgreSQL schema definitions into projects, folders, and files. It works like a lightweight version of VS Code's Explorer sidebar — with drag-and-drop reordering, instant file search, and full cloud sync for team projects.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [SQL Editor Overview](/sql-editor) · [Collaboration](/team-collaboration)

![The SQL Editor in fullscreen mode showing the active fi
items.snippet: The SQL Editor's file manager lets you organize your PostgreSQL schema definitions into projects, folders, and files. It works like a lightweight version of VS Code's Explorer sidebar — with drag-and-

items.title: SQL Editor
items.description: Write, organize, and analyze PostgreSQL schemas in the Schema Weaver SQL Editor — the primary workspace for schema development

headings.title: What can you do in the SQL Editor?

items.content:
SQL Editor

The SQL Editor is the primary workspace in Schema Weaver. It's where you write PostgreSQL schema definitions, organize them into projects and files, analyze your schema quality with the built-in compiler, and visualize table relationships as an interactive ER diagram — all in one place.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Docs Home](https://docs.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Data Explorer](https://docs.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/data-explor
items.snippet: The SQL Editor is the primary workspace in Schema Weaver. It's where you write PostgreSQL schema definitions, organize them into projects and files, analyze your schema quality with the built-in compi

items.title: Schema Compiler
items.description: Understand the SQL Editor's 20-layer schema compiler — how it grades your PostgreSQL schema, surfaces issues, and helps you write production-ready DDL
items.content:
Schema Compiler

The Schema Compiler is a static analysis engine built into the SQL Editor. Every time you edit a file, it silently re-compiles the merged project SQL through 20 analysis layers and produces a health grade with a detailed issue report — all in your browser, with no server round-trip.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [SQL Editor Overview](/sql-editor) · [Writing SQL](/sql-editor/writing-sql)

---

Opening the Compiler Panel

Click Compil
items.snippet: The Schema Compiler is a static analysis engine built into the SQL Editor. Every time you edit a file, it silently re-compiles the merged project SQL through 20 analysis layers and produces a health g

items.title: Schema Diff
items.description: Compare two versions of a PostgreSQL schema side-by-side with the SQL Editor's built-in diff tool — highlight additions, removals, and modifications
items.content:
Schema Diff

The Schema Diff tool lets you compare two PostgreSQL schema states side-by-side. It highlights every added, removed, and modified object — tables, columns, indexes, constraints, and more — so you can review changes before applying a migration or audit what drifted in production.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [SQL Editor Overview](/sql-editor) · [Version History](/sql-editor/version-history)

---

Opening the Diff Panel

Click Diff in th
items.snippet: The Schema Diff tool lets you compare two PostgreSQL schema states side-by-side. It highlights every added, removed, and modified object — tables, columns, indexes, constraints, and more — so you can

items.title: Version History
items.description: Browse and restore every saved snapshot of your SQL files in the Schema Weaver SQL Editor — never lose a working schema state
items.content:
Version History

The SQL Editor keeps a complete snapshot history of every file in your cloud projects. Every save creates an immutable version entry — you can browse the timeline, compare any two snapshots, or restore a previous state with a single click.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [SQL Editor Overview](/sql-editor) · [Schema Diff](/sql-editor/schema-diff)

---

Opening Version History

There are two ways to open the version history panel:

1. R
items.snippet: The SQL Editor keeps a complete snapshot history of every file in your cloud projects. Every save creates an immutable version entry — you can browse the timeline, compare any two snapshots, or restor

items.title: Writing SQL
items.description: Use the SQL Editor's tabs, syntax highlighting, autosave, split view, and fullscreen mode to write PostgreSQL schema definitions efficiently
items.content:
Writing SQL

The SQL Editor gives you a PostgreSQL-aware code editor with multi-file tabs, syntax highlighting, autosave, and a fullscreen mode. This page covers everything about the editing experience itself.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [SQL Editor Overview](/sql-editor) · [Schema Compiler](/sql-editor/schema-compiler)

![The Schema Weaver SQL Editor showing the file tabs, PostgreSQL syntax highlighting, Explorer sidebar, and status bar](/images/
items.snippet: The SQL Editor gives you a PostgreSQL-aware code editor with multi-file tabs, syntax highlighting, autosave, and a fullscreen mode. This page covers everything about the editing experience itself.

items.title: How Resona AI Works
items.description: Understand the agentic loop, tool execution pipeline, and the full set of analysis tools Resona uses to answer your data questions
items.content:
How Resona AI Works

Resona AI is not a simple chatbot. It is an agentic system — when you ask a question, it breaks the problem down into steps, calls specialized tools to gather and analyze data, and synthesizes the results into a complete answer. You can watch every step happen in real time.

> Quick links: [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [AI Chat Interface](/data-explorer/ai-chat-interface) · [Charts & Analysis Results](/data-explorer/ai-artifacts)

![Resona
items.snippet: Resona AI is not a simple chatbot. It is an **agentic system** — when you ask a question, it breaks the problem down into steps, calls specialized tools to gather and analyze data, and synthesizes the

items.title: Charts & Analysis Results
items.description: Understand the artifact types Resona AI generates — charts, stat blocks, and data tables — and how to interact with them
items.content:
Charts & Analysis Results

When Resona AI finishes an analysis, it outputs artifacts — rich interactive components embedded directly in the chat. These are not static images. You can expand them, sort them, pin them to a dashboard, and export their underlying SQL.

> Quick links: [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [AI Chat Interface](/data-explorer/ai-chat-interface) · [Workspace & Projects](/data-explorer/ai-workspace)

![Resona AI chat response with an expanded i
items.snippet: When Resona AI finishes an analysis, it outputs **artifacts** — rich interactive components embedded directly in the chat. These are not static images. You can expand them, sort them, pin them to a da

items.title: AI Chat Interface
items.description: Use Resona AI inside the Data Explorer to query, analyze, and visualize your data using natural language
items.content:
AI Chat Interface

Resona AI is the agentic assistant built directly into the Data Explorer. You interact with it through a chat panel — type a question in plain English, and Resona queries your database, runs analysis tools, and returns results as interactive charts, tables, and reports.

> Quick links: [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Data Explorer Overview](https://docs.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/data-explorer/) · [How Resona AI Works](/data-explorer/agentic-w
items.snippet: Resona AI is the agentic assistant built directly into the Data Explorer. You interact with it through a chat panel — type a question in plain English, and Resona queries your database, runs analysis

items.title: Resona AI
items.description: Overview of Resona AI inside the Data Explorer — what it can do, how to access it, and where to go next

headings.title: What can Resona do inside Data Explorer?
How do you access Resona AI?
Which pages should you read next?

items.content:
Resona AI

Resona AI is the agentic data assistant built into the Data Explorer. You describe what you want to know in plain English — Resona figures out which tables to query, runs the analysis, and returns the results as interactive charts, tables, and reports.

> Quick links: [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Data Explorer Overview](https://docs.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/data-explorer/) · [SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/)

![Resona AI pane

items.title: Workspace & Projects
items.description: Manage generated reports, organize analysis into projects, and browse your full artifact history in the Data Explorer
items.content:
Workspace & Projects

Resona AI stores everything it generates. The Workspace holds exported files, Projects organize your analysis sessions, and Artifact History gives you a searchable archive of every chart and result ever produced.

> Quick links: [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [AI Chat Interface](/data-explorer/ai-chat-interface) · [Data Explorer Overview](https://docs.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/data-explorer/)

![Resona AI interface showing the workspace a
items.snippet: Resona AI stores everything it generates. The **Workspace** holds exported files, **Projects** organize your analysis sessions, and **Artifact History** gives you a searchable archive of every chart a

items.title: Exploring Your Data
items.description: Browse tables, inspect columns, filter rows, and analyze column statistics in the Data Explorer grid
items.content:
Exploring Your Data

Once you've selected a PostgreSQL connection, the interface gives you a two-panel layout for browsing live data — a sidebar of tables on the left and a high-performance data grid on the right. Connections are created in the [SQL Editor](/database/adding-a-connection) and are available here automatically — no separate setup needed.

> Quick links: [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Data Explorer Overview](https://docs.schemaweaver.vivekmind.

items.title: Exporting Data
items.description: Export individual tables or entire database schemas to CSV, JSON, NDJSON, Excel, and SQL formats
items.content:
Exporting Data

The Data Explorer lets you export data in multiple formats — either a single table or your entire database schema as a ZIP archive. Exports are triggered directly from the browser and download instantly.

> Quick links: [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Data Explorer Overview](https://docs.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/data-explorer/) · [SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/)

---

Export Button

items.title: Data Explorer
items.description: Query your PostgreSQL databases interactively with the Schema Weaver Data Explorer

headings.title: What can you do in Data Explorer?
Which databases does Data Explorer support today?
How do you access Data Explorer?

items.content:
Data Explorer

The Data Explorer is a built-in SQL workbench that lets you connect to your databases, run queries, and explore your data — all inside Schema Weaver. It integrates with Resona AI so you can ask questions in plain English and get runnable SQL back instantly.

> Quick links: [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Docs Home](https://docs.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Schema Weaver Landing](https://schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [SQL Editor](https://sq

items.title: AI Chat Guide
items.description: How to write effective prompts, use @mentions, iterate on results, and get the best answers from Resona AI in both the SQL Editor and Data Explorer
items.content:
AI Chat Guide

Resona AI is available in two surfaces — the SQL Editor and the Data Explorer. Both share the same chat mechanics: a streaming conversation with tool execution, model selection, and full context of your schema. This page covers how to use the chat interface effectively in both places.

---

Opening the Chat

SQL Editor

Click Resona AI in the left sidebar panel of the SQL Editor. The panel stays open as you navigate files and maintains your full conversation history. You can also
items.snippet: Resona AI is available in two surfaces — the SQL Editor and the Data Explorer. Both share the same chat mechanics: a streaming conversation with tool execution, model selection, and full context of yo

items.title: Resona AI
items.description: Resona is Schema Weaver's AI assistant — available in both the SQL Editor and the Data Explorer with purpose-built architectures for schema design and live data analysis
items.content:
Resona AI

Resona is Schema Weaver's AI assistant. It is embedded in two products — the SQL Editor and the Data Explorer — each with a purpose-built architecture for its context. Both surfaces use the same model selection, the same credit system, and the same account.

---

Two Products, One AI

Resona in the SQL Editor

The SQL Editor's Resona AI is an agentic schema assistant. It has access to your project files, your compiled schema graph, your ER diagram, and 55 purpose-built tools for writi
items.snippet: Resona is Schema Weaver's AI assistant. It is embedded in two products — the **SQL Editor** and the **Data Explorer** — each with a purpose-built architecture for its context. Both surfaces use the sa

items.title: Models and Credits
items.description: All AI models available in Resona — four tiers (FAST, SMART, ADVANCED, POWERFUL), credit costs per model, plan limits, and how to choose the right model

headings.title: What You Can Do With Each Plan

items.content:
Models and Credits

Resona AI supports a large range of models from multiple providers, organized into four capability tiers. You choose the model per message — use a FAST model for quick questions and a POWERFUL model for complex multi-table design or deep analysis. Credits are shared across the SQL Editor and Data Explorer.

---

Model Tiers

Models are grouped into four tiers based on capability and cost. The credit multiplier shown in the UI (e.g. `1.0×`) is relative to the base rate of the
items.snippet: Resona AI supports a large range of models from multiple providers, organized into four capability tiers. You choose the model per message — use a FAST model for quick questions and a POWERFUL model f

items.title: Team Collaboration
items.description: Create teams, invite members, connect projects, and share database access — all managed from Settings → Collaboration in Schema Weaver
items.content:
Team Collaboration

Schema Weaver's collaboration model is built around Teams. You create a team, invite members by email, and connect your projects to that team. Everyone on the team gets access to the connected projects — and the database connections linked to those projects — without any credential sharing.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/)

---

Where to Find Team Settings

All team mana
items.snippet: Schema Weaver's collaboration model is built around **Teams**. You create a team, invite members by email, and connect your projects to that team. Everyone on the team gets access to the connected pro
